{{Quote|Personally I wasn't all that impressed with the Sharu. No sense of humor or style. Nonetheless, there Elthree and I were, deep in their sacred temple. And that's when I saw it.|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="Solo Expanded" />}}
The '''Mindharp of Sharu''' was an item involved in [[Raid on the sacred temple of Sharu|one]] of [[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian's]] adventures prior to his [[Raid on Kessel|adventure]] with [[Han Solo]] in [[10 BBY]]. Calrissian told an exaggerated account of the caper as part of his ''[[Calrissian Chronicles]]'' stories.<ref name="Solo Official Guide" /> He and his [[droid]] partner [[L3-37]] got to see the Mindharp deep in<ref name="Solo Expanded" /> the [[sacred temple of Sharu]].<ref name="Book" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:The_Mindharp_of_Sharu.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu, as depicted in ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'']]
The Mindharp of Sharu was introduced into the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the [[2018]] reference book ''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide]]'' by [[Pablo Hidalgo]].<ref name="Solo Official Guide" /> The [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]] originated in the [[1983]] [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] novel ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', part of [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy. In that story, it was a legendary tool used by a race known as the [[Sharu/Legends|Toka]] who were known called the Sharu, which would allow them to reclaim their knowledge of their former civilization after a threat to their world had passed.<ref name="Mindharp of Sharu">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref>
